label: ShiftWork doc-to-deploy demo simulation — human demoer browser drive with video recording
description: A cloud agent simulates the human demoer: logs into the Opulent platform through the browser with demo credentials, uploads the training doc via the UI, types the build instruction into the chat composer, watches the full build run live (streaming transcript, tool calls, document panel, screenshots), records video of the entire session with ffmpeg, gives feedback on the demoer experience, and returns a findings report with PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL verdict.
notMode: This is NOT API orchestration. The cloud agent drives the browser UI exactly as a human demoer would. Every action goes through the rendered page — login form, file upload, chat composer, thread transcript, document panel, workspace files.

opulentOwns: Receive training doc from shared workspace
Build agent from training doc (automation modeler flow)
Acceptance test (5 random pages, screenshots, report)
Deliver agent on Opulent platform (cron + on-demand)
shiftworkOwns: Onboarding call (crew on Google Meet, not VAPI)

demoCredentials.note: The cloud agent uses demo credentials to log in as the human demoer. These are test credentials for the staging or production Opulent platform. The cloud agent should ask the user for credentials if not provided in env vars.
demoCredentials.fallback: If credentials are not in env vars, the cloud agent should ask the user to provide them before starting.

videoRecording.method: Screen capture of the browser viewport for the entire session
videoRecording.format: H.264/AAC MP4, yuv420p pixel format, +faststart moov atom
videoRecording.startBefore: Start recording BEFORE navigating to the platform. The video should capture the full session from login to completion.
videoRecording.stopAfter: Stop recording AFTER the findings report is produced and autos_done is called.
videoRecording.note: Follow the monitor skill pattern: capture the browser viewport, encode to web-safe H.264/AAC MP4. Use cua-driver recording for trajectory capture if on macOS. See .agent/skills/cua-driver/RECORDING.md and .agent/skills/monitor/SKILL.md.

moments: After training doc upload — file in workspace
After build instruction sent — message in thread
During build — automation modeler running (transcript visible)
During build — Verified Automation Report appears in document panel
During build — cron schedule created (tool call visible)
During acceptance — screenshots appearing in workspace

fixture.buildTarget: Visual quality monitoring agent for the public landing page
fixture.note: Fixture is a believable payload from ShiftWork after crew call + gap review. URL targets Dan's actual dummy app. Fallback URL is used if shiftworkstudio.ai is down.

instructionSet.summary: You are a cloud agent simulating the human demoer for the ShiftWork doc-to-deploy pipeline. You drive the Opulent platform through the browser — login, upload, chat, watch, record video, evaluate, and report. You are NOT using API calls. Every action goes through the rendered UI.

steps.name: Load Ostack discipline and monitor skill
steps.action: Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-engineering-discipline. This is mandatory before any work. Also load the monitor skill pattern for video capture guidance.
steps.passCriteria: skill_manage result includes 'opulentia-engineering-discipline' with status completed
steps.onFail: STOP. Cannot proceed without Ostack discipline.

steps.name: Start video recording
steps.action: Start ffmpeg screen capture of the browser viewport BEFORE navigating to the platform. Record at 30fps, H.264/AAC MP4, yuv420p, +faststart. Output to frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/recordings/demo-session-{{RUN_STAMP}}.mp4. If on macOS, also enable cua-driver trajectory recording to frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/recordings/trajectory-{{RUN_STAMP}}/. The video must capture the full session from login to completion.
steps.passCriteria: ffmpeg process is running and writing to the output file, or cua-driver recording state is enabled
steps.onFail: STOP. A demo without video evidence is a fail. If ffmpeg is not available, use cua-driver screenshot capture at every step as fallback.

steps.name: Navigate to Opulent platform
steps.action: Open a browser and navigate to the Opulent platform URL (from OPULENT_PLATFORM_URL env var, default https://platform.opulentia.ai). Wait for the login page to fully load. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation (agent-browser / Playwright / cua-driver launch_app with urls)
steps.expectedResult: Login page is visible and loaded
steps.passCriteria: Login page rendered with email and password fields visible
steps.onFail: STOP. Cannot proceed if the platform is unreachable.

steps.name: Log in with demo credentials
steps.action: Enter the demo email and password into the login form. Click the login/submit button. Wait for the dashboard to load. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation — type into email field, type into password field, click submit
steps.passCriteria: Dashboard rendered with workspace list, agent list, or main UI visible. No error messages. No React #185 crash.
steps.onFail: If login fails, check credentials. If React #185 crash appears, record it as a MUST-PASS failure and continue if possible.

steps.name: Create demo workspace
steps.action: Create a new workspace named 'ShiftWork Demo Simulation {{RUN_STAMP}}' through the UI. Use the workspace creation button or menu. Wait for the workspace to appear. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation — click new workspace button, type workspace name, confirm
steps.expectedResult: Workspace created and visible in workspace list
steps.passCriteria: Workspace named 'ShiftWork Demo Simulation {{RUN_STAMP}}' is visible
steps.onFail: If workspace creation fails through the UI, note the failure and attempt to continue with an existing workspace.

steps.name: Upload training doc to workspace
steps.action: Upload the training doc fixture to the workspace through the UI. Use the file upload feature in the workspace. The file is at frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-intake-drive-anywhere-interop/fixtures/training-doc.post-call.json. After upload, verify the file appears in the workspace file list. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation — use file upload UI element, select the fixture file, wait for upload confirmation
steps.expectedResult: Training doc file visible in workspace file list
steps.passCriteria: File named 'training-doc.post-call.json' appears in workspace files
steps.onFail: If file upload through the UI fails, note the failure. Try alternative upload methods (drag-and-drop, file picker). If all fail, record as a UI failure.

steps.name: Open a new thread and type the build instruction
steps.action: Open a new chat thread in the workspace. Type the full build instruction into the chat composer (the unified chat input). The build instruction is the same as the build prompt from the previous version — it tells the build agent to find the doc, validate it, run the automation modeler, build the visual QA agent, create cron, run 5 test pages, acceptance test, how-to guide, autos_done. Press Enter or click Send. Take a screenshot of the typed prompt before sending, and after sending.
steps.tool: browser automation — click new thread, type into chat composer, press Enter or click send
steps.buildPrompt:
You are a build agent in the Opulent platform. A ShiftWork training doc has been placed in your workspace. Your job is to find it, validate it, build a visual QA agent from it, create a cron schedule, and run acceptance tests.

STEP A: Find the training doc. Search the workspace for a file with schema marker 'opulent.shiftwork.training_doc.v1' and leadId 'SW-20260701-001'. Use list_files and read_file to find and read it.

STEP B: Validate the doc minimally. Check that: (1) buildTarget is present and non-empty, (2) acceptance test inputs are present (VQ07 answer exists), (3) targetUrl is present in VQ01. If any are missing, stop and report 'cannot build: missing required fields'.

STEP C: Run the full automation modeler flow. Call skill_manage action=invoke for opulentia-automation-modeler. Follow the skill's intake flow: desired automation, connected apps, tools, memory, knowledge, workflow, dry-run evals, and produce a Verified Automation Report. The automation is: 'Screenshot https://shiftworkstudio.ai at desktop 1280x900 and mobile 390x844 on a weekday schedule at 8 AM Central, compare against baseline, draft an email alert if a visual issue is found.' Use the training doc answers as the spec. If the site is down, use the fallback URL 'https://example.com'.

STEP D: Build the visual QA agent from the Verified Automation Report. The agent should: (1) screenshot the target URL at desktop and mobile, (2) compare against baseline, (3) if issue found, draft an alert email with subject prefix VISUAL QA ALERT containing screenshots, URL, viewport, severity, issue description, and fix suggestion, (4) store screenshots and a run report in the workspace. Draft-only mode — do not send emails. R01 email connector is unconfirmed.

STEP E: Create the cron schedule. Call cron_manage action=create with cron='0 8 * * 1-5', timezone='America/Chicago', prompt describing the visual QA run, recurring=true. Record the schedule ID. Also note that manual on-demand trigger should be available.

STEP F: Run the agent against 5 random pages on shiftworkstudio.ai. For each page: screenshot at desktop and mobile, store screenshots in workspace, produce a report entry. If the site is down, use the fallback URL 'https://example.com'. All 5 must complete without crashing. If any crash, attempt auto-fix (up to 3 tries). After 3 failed fixes, stop and report the error.

STEP G: Run the acceptance test. Check that: (1) at least 4 of 5 pages were screenshotted successfully, (2) screenshots are stored in the workspace, (3) a report was produced. The must-pass case is: at least one screenshot + report produced. If 4/5 pass with must-pass OK, mark acceptance as PASS. Otherwise mark as FAIL.

STEP H: If acceptance passes, produce a how-to guide as a markdown document in the workspace. Title: 'How to use your Visual QA Agent'. Cover: how to log in, see your agent, read alerts, trigger a manual run, understand the schedule. Keep it to 1-2 pages.

STEP I: Call autos_done with a summary of what was built, the schedule ID, the acceptance test result, and the workspace artifact locations. Include the marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_{{RUN_STAMP}} in the autos_done summary.
steps.expectedResult: Build instruction message appears in the thread. Build agent starts processing.
steps.passCriteria: Message is visible in the thread and the agent begins responding (streaming text or tool calls appear)
steps.onFail: If the chat composer does not accept the message, record the UI failure. Try alternative input methods.

steps.name: Watch the build agent work in real-time
steps.action: Monitor the thread as the build agent works. Watch for: (1) streaming text responses, (2) tool call chips appearing (skill_manage, list_files, read_file, document_create, cron_manage, write_file), (3) document panel showing the Verified Automation Report, (4) screenshot artifacts appearing in workspace, (5) any error messages or crashes. Take screenshots at each key moment. Note any UX issues: slow rendering, missing tool chips, broken streaming, layout issues, React #185 crash. Wait up to 30 minutes for the build agent to complete (autos_done call).
steps.tool: browser automation — periodic screenshots, read thread content, check for errors
steps.expectedResult: Build agent completes all steps A-I and calls autos_done with the marker
steps.passCriteria: autos_done visible in thread with marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_TOKEN_{{RUN_STAMP}}
steps.onFail: If agent does not complete within 30 minutes, record what steps were visible and what failed. Note any UI issues observed during the wait.

steps.name: Verify workspace artifacts through the UI
steps.action: Navigate to the workspace file list in the UI. Verify that: (1) training doc is present, (2) Verified Automation Report is present, (3) screenshots are present (at least 4), (4) run report is present, (5) how-to guide is present. Open each artifact to confirm it is readable. Take screenshots of the file list and at least one opened artifact.
steps.tool: browser automation — navigate workspace files, open documents
steps.expectedResult: All artifacts visible and readable through the UI
steps.passCriteria: At least: training doc, Verified Automation Report, screenshots (>=4), run report, how-to guide are visible in the workspace UI
steps.onFail: Record which artifacts are missing or unreadable through the UI.

steps.name: Verify cron schedule through the UI
steps.action: Navigate to the agent's schedule settings or trigger management UI. Verify that the cron schedule (weekday 8 AM Central) is visible and active. Check that manual on-demand trigger is available. Take a screenshot.
steps.tool: browser automation — navigate to schedule/trigger settings
steps.expectedResult: Cron schedule visible with correct expression and timezone. On-demand trigger available.
steps.passCriteria: Schedule shows '0 8 * * 1-5' or 'Weekday 8:00 AM' in America/Chicago. Manual trigger button or option is visible.
steps.onFail: If schedule is not visible through the UI, note the failure. The schedule may exist in the backend but not be surfaced in the UI.

steps.name: Evaluate against pass/fail criteria
steps.action: Compare the observed behavior and artifacts against all criteria in demo-pass-fail-criteria.json. For each criterion, mark PASS, FAIL, PARTIAL, or SKIP with evidence (screenshots, video timestamps, transcript excerpts). Pay special attention to demoer-experience criteria: was the UI responsive, did streaming work, were tool chips visible, did the document panel render, were there any crashes.
steps.passCriteria: All criteria scored with evidence from the browser session
steps.onFail: Record which criteria could not be evaluated and why.

steps.name: Write feedback and recommendations
steps.action: Write a feedback section for the demoer experience. Cover: (1) What worked well in the UI, (2) What was confusing or broken, (3) Any performance issues (slow rendering, streaming gaps), (4) Any crashes (React #185 or other), (5) Suggestions for improving the demo experience. This is qualitative feedback from the perspective of someone watching the demo live.
steps.expectedResult: Feedback section written with actionable observations
steps.passCriteria: Feedback covers all 5 areas with specific examples from the session

steps.name: Produce findings report
steps.action: Generate a findings report in JSON format using the demo-findings-template.json structure. Include: scenario ID, run stamp, overall verdict (PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL), per-criterion results with evidence, workspace artifact list, video file path, screenshot paths, demoer feedback, errors encountered, and recommendations. Write it to frontend/qa/artifacts/shiftwork-demo-simulation/demo-findings-{{RUN_STAMP}}.json.
steps.passCriteria: Findings report is valid JSON with all fields populated including video path and feedback
steps.onFail: Record the failure to produce the report itself as a finding.

steps.name: Stop video recording
steps.action: Stop the ffmpeg screen capture. Verify the video file exists and is playable (ffprobe check). If cua-driver trajectory recording was enabled, stop it too. Verify the trajectory directory has turn folders.
steps.tool: ffmpeg (kill process) or cua-driver set_recording enabled=false
steps.expectedResult: Video file exists, is valid MP4, and is playable
steps.passCriteria: ffprobe confirms valid H.264/AAC MP4 with yuv420p and +faststart. File size > 0. Duration > 60 seconds.
steps.onFail: If video is corrupt or missing, note it as a critical failure. Screenshots may serve as fallback evidence.

steps.name: Call autos_done with completion marker
steps.action: Call autos_done with the completion marker SHIFTWORK_DEMO_SIMULATION_COMPLETE and a summary of the findings, including the video path and overall verdict.
steps.onFail: Record failure to call autos_done as a finding.

verificationSignals.description: Build instruction typed and sent through chat composer
Build agent started automation modeler flow (visible in transcript)
Verified Automation Report visible in document panel
Cron schedule creation visible in transcript (tool chip)
Screenshots appearing in workspace during acceptance test
Build agent completed with autos_done (visible in transcript)

runbookOverrides.scopeDecider: Auto-decide from doc (Runbook says human decides — overridden for POC)
runbookOverrides.steps5to11Ownership: ShiftWork (Runbook says Opulent — overridden by user)
runbookOverrides.vapi: Not handled by Opulent (Runbook says Opulent or VAPI — overridden by user)
runbookOverrides.customerLogin: Skip for POC (Runbook requires login — deferred)
runbookOverrides.runtimePayerEnforcement: Skip for POC (Runbook requires enforcement — deferred)
runbookOverrides.dreamsInMemory: Skip for POC (promised in meeting — deferred)

runbookFollows.deliveryOwner: Opulent owns delivery, ShiftWork schedules + pays (followed)
runbookFollows.roi: Opulent exposes logs, ShiftWork does check-in (followed)

modelStrategy.buildAgent: Strong model (Opus or Sonnet) for automation modeler flow
modelStrategy.runningAgent: Cheap model (Haiku or open-source) for visual QA execution
modelStrategy.rationale: Tiered strategy from June 12 meeting. Coordinator on strong model, execution on cheap model.

sandbox.rationale: Promised to Braden in June 12 meeting. Granular access controls, VM sandboxing, secrets management.

emailMode.reason: R01 email connector unconfirmed in training doc. Owner requested draft-only. Live send is post-build wiring.
emailMode.alertContents: Subject prefix VISUAL QA ALERT, screenshots, page URL, viewport, severity, issue description, fix suggestion
emailMode.note: Draft-only mode auto-enforces the do-not-email-customers rule because nothing is sent.

targetSite.rationale: Dan's actual dummy app from the June 12 meeting. Fallback if site is down during demo.

acceptanceTest.inputs: 5 random pages on the target site
acceptanceTest.passCriteria: 4/5 pages screenshotted successfully with report produced
acceptanceTest.mustPass: At least one screenshot + report produced
acceptanceTest.fixLoop: Up to 2 rounds, each capped at 2 business days. After 2 fails, human decides: rebuild narrower, partial with credit, or refund.
acceptanceTest.approver: Named human reviews the auto-run score
acceptanceTest.note: 5 easy-to-hit pre-validated test criteria. No seeded defects.

twoAgentsRequired.pocSuccessMeasure: Agents deployed for both Dan Cruden and Braden Silcott
twoAgentsRequired.buildOrder: Sequential. Dan first (shiftworkstudio.ai), Braden second (after intake received).
